title: The Law on the Restructuring of Certain Receivables and Amending Certain Laws has been published
bodyHtml:
The Law on the Restructuring of Certain Receivables and Amending Certain Laws was published on the Turkish Official Gazette on 12 March 2023. The law includes provisions regarding the restructuring of various public receivables, including customs receivables.

 Customs penalties, administrative fines, interests, delay interest, and late fee receivables whose customs liability arising before the date of 31/12/2022 (including this date) will be benefited from the restructuring.

According to the Law restructuring shall be implemented as follows;

1. Finalized customs receivables

Regarding tax receivables not paid on time and tax receivables whose payment period has not yet expired as of the promulgation date of the Law:

-If the taxpayer pays the entire customs duty as well as the amount to be calculated based on the Producer Price Index (PPI) monthly rates until the promulgation of the Law and in accordance with the Law (if the unpaid receivable only consists of delay interest, the amount calculated, instead of delay interest, based on monthly PPI rates), the entire delay interests and administrative fines will be written off.

-If the taxpayer pays 50 percent of administrative fines not derived from a customs duty or tax penalty arising from the participation provisions of Misdemeanor Law No. 5326 and the amount to be calculated based on the PPI monthly rates until the promulgation of the Law and in accordance with the Law, the remaining 50 percent of the penalty will be written off.

-If the taxpayer pays 30 percent of administrative fines issued depending on the customs value of the goods and the entire customs duty and delay interests as well as the amount to be calculated based on the PPI monthly rates until the promulgation of the Law and in accordance with the Law, the remaining 70 percent of the penalty and the entire delay interests will be written off.

2. Customs receivables that are not finalized or are in litigation

a. Accruals related to customs duties in litigation before first-degree courts or whose deadline for filing a lawsuit has not expired as of the promulgation of the Law

If the taxpayer pays 50 percent of the customs duty as well as the amount to be calculated on 50 percent of the customs duty amount based on the PPI monthly rates until the promulgation of the Law and in accordance with the Law, the remaining 50 percent of the customs duty amount and the entire tax penalty (including delay interests and other penalties arising from the customs duty as well as delay interests related to these penalties) will be written off.

If the taxpayer pays 15 percent of administrative fines issued based on the customs value of the goods in accordance with the Law, the remaining penalty will be written off.

Customs duties that have been filed to administrative appeals fall within the scope above, provided the deadline for filing an administrative appeal has not expired.

b. Accruals related to customs duties whose deadline for filing an appeal or objection has not expired, that are in appeal or in correction of decision, or whose deadline for the correction of the decision has not expired

In these situations, the amounts written off depend on the last court decision rendered before the promulgation date of the Law as to the cancellation, approval or reversal. In this regard:

-Where the last decision cancels the tax assessments, if 10 percent of the customs duty and the amount to be calculated, instead of delay interest, over 10 percent of the customs duty based on monthly D-PPI rates applied until the promulgation date of the Law are paid within the periods and procedures stipulated in the Law, the remaining 90 percent of the tax principal and the entire tax penalties (as well as the remaining 90 percent of the penalties that are not derived from a tax principal if the remaining 10 percent thereof is paid) will be written off. If the taxpayer pays 5 percent of administrative fines issued based on the customs value of the goods in accordance with the Law, the remaining penalty will be written off.

-Where the last decision approves the tax assessments or approves them with amendment, if the entire approved customs duty, 10 percent of the reversed customs duty and the amount to be calculated, instead of delay interest, based on monthly PPI rates applied until the promulgation date of the Law are paid within the periods and procedures stipulated in the Law, the remaining 90 percent of the customs duty and the entire tax penalties (as well as the remaining part of the penalties that are not derived from a tax principal if 50 percent of the approved amount and 10 percent of the reversed amount thereof are paid) will be written off. If the taxpayer pays 30 percent of the approved fine amount and 5 percent of the reversed amount related to the administrative fines issued based on the customs value of the goods in accordance with the Law, the remaining penalty will be written off.

-Where the last decision reverses the decision, if 50 percent of the customs duty and the amount to be calculated, instead of delay interest, over 50 percent of the customs duty based on monthly D-PPI rates applied until the promulgation date of the Law are paid within the periods and procedures stipulated in the Law, the remaining 50 percent of the tax principal and the entire tax penalties (as well as the remaining 75 percent of the penalties that are not derived from a tax principal if 25 percent thereof is paid) will be written off. If the taxpayer pays 15 percent of administrative fines issued based on the customs value of the goods in accordance with the Law, the remaining penalty will be written off.

-If the last decision is on a partial approval and partial reversal:

  a.For the approved part, if the entire approved customs duty, 10 percent of the reversed customs duty and the amount to be calculated, instead of delay interest, based on monthly D-PPI rates applied until the promulgation date of the Law are paid within the periods and procedures stipulated in the Law, the remaining 90 percent of the customs duty and the entire tax penalties (as well as 50 percent of the penalties that are not derived from a tax principal if the remaining part thereof is paid) will be written off.

b.For the reversed part, if 50 percent of the customs duty and the amount to be calculated, instead of delay interest, over 50 percent of the customs duty based on monthly D-PPI rates applied until the promulgation date of the Law are paid within the periods and procedures stipulated in the Law, the remaining 50 percent of the customs duty and the entire tax penalties (as well as 75 percent of the penalties that are not derived from a tax principal if the 25 percent thereof is paid) will be written off.

c.If the taxpayer pays 30 percent of the approved fine amount, 5 percent of the reversed amount and 15 percent of the reversed part related to the administrative fines issued based on the customs value of the goods in accordance with the Law, the remaining penalty will be written off

3. Customs receivables under customs inspection or assessment

Customs inspections and assessments that were initialized but are incomplete by the promulgation date of the Law will continue to be carried out. Once these customs duty assessments have been completed, if the taxpayer pays the first 50 percent of the customs duty and the amount to be calculated on 50 percent of the customs duty based on the PPI monthly rates until the promulgation of the Law and in accordance with the Law, the remaining 50 percent of the customs duty, delay interests and the entire tax penalty (for penalties depending on the customs value of the goods if the 15 percent of the penalty is paid, the remaining 85 percent) will be written off.

4. Payment methods

To benefit from the restructuring opportunity, taxpayers should apply to the related customs authority by 31 May 2023.
